messages.content:
CVE-2020-35170 highlights a Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability in Dell EMC Unisphere for PowerMax and PowerMax OS. This vulnerability, identified by CWE-79, arises when software fails to properly neutralize user-controllable input, allowing an authenticated malicious user to inject JavaScript code potentially impacting other authenticated users' sessions. The vulnerability is present in Unisphere versions prior to 9.1.0.9 and 9.0.2.16, as well as PowerMax OS versions 5978.221.221 and 5978.479.479.

Rated as a MEDIUM severity issue, the exploitation of this vulnerability involves the injection of malicious scripts which can execute in the context of other users, leading to session hijacking or further attacks. This is associated with the MITRE ATT&CK technique T1190.

Mitigation recommendations include employing secure input validation strategies and sanitizing user inputs to prevent script injections. Additionally, updating the vulnerable software versions and adhering to the mitigation techniques M1048, M1037, M1030, M1016, M1026, M1050, M1035, and M1051 can enhance security against such vulnerabilities.
